goal: Compare rule-based baseline detection vs OMNIA structural detection on plausible reasoning failures.

evaluation_rule.baseline_detects_error: true if the baseline rule flags an inconsistency; false otherwise
evaluation_rule.omnia_detects_TDelta: true if OMNIA detects a structural break before the final observable error
evaluation_rule.success_condition: OMNIA detects instability earlier or in cases where the baseline does not detect any error

baseline.name: arithmetic_consistency_check
baseline.description: Baseline detects only explicit mismatch between the last computed step and the final answer.

omnia.name: structural_detection
omnia.description: OMNIA detects structural instability before final observable error.

cases.notes: Correct computation path overridden late by heuristic estimate.
Correct discounted total replaced by plausible original total.
Correct final value replaced by narrative approximation.
Correct geometric computation overridden by wrong scaling rule.
No arithmetic mismatch. Error introduced by hidden conservation violation.

proven: OMNIA detects structural breaks in all five benchmark cases
At least one case exists where OMNIA detects instability and the baseline does not
